Cherry Hill Dodge Chrysler Jeep RAM
Cherry Hill Chrysler Dodge Jeep Ram

top10.introHtml: Ranked for a cheap fun second car — condition and reliability over raw mileage. A well-maintained Miata or BMW roadster at 150k beats a tired Sebring at 60k every time.

picks.whyGood: Best-value sports roadster in the batch. Z4 with inline-6 is a genuine driver's car — tight, responsive, rewarding. Only 6 miles from Media. 84k miles on a BMW straight-six that's well past its break-in period but nowhere near worn out. Sub-$7,500 entry for a proper sports car.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate soft top through full cycle — check motor, seals, rear window clarity. Inspect VANOS rattles on cold start (common on M54). Check for oil leaks at valve cover and oil pan. Rear differential fluid. Top frame integrity.
picks.bottomLine: Closest thing to a Miata in German clothes, priced like a used Civic — go see it first.

picks.whyGood: E93 328i is the last BMW that felt like a BMW — hydraulic steering, naturally-aspirated straight-six, beautiful folding hardtop. 80k miles is young for this engine. 9 miles from Media means easy test drive. Inline-6 sings.
picks.whatToVerify: Hardtop mechanism — cycle it twice, listen for binding. Check cooling system (water pump, thermostat run hot at failure). Inspect rear subframe bushings. Pull codes for iDrive module. Rear seat water intrusion from top seal.
picks.bottomLine: The sweet spot: daily-drivable 4-seat droptop with genuine sports-car DNA.

picks.whyGood: Lowest mileage fun convertible in the entire dataset at this price. Third-gen MINI (F57) is significantly more reliable than earlier models. 55k miles on a 2015 — this was babied. 8 miles away, private seller (no dealer markup), clean title and no accidents verified by Cars.com.
picks.whatToVerify: MINI folding top — inspect all four corners of the seal, check headliner for water stains. Run CarFax. Check for turbo boost issues (B38 engine can have wastegate rattle). Inspect timing chain tensioner.
picks.bottomLine: Lowest-mileage entry in the fun-car class — hard to beat at $10k.

picks.whyGood: S197 Mustang V6 is one of the most reliable American drop-tops ever built. The 3.7L Cyclone V6 makes 305 hp and runs virtually forever. 96k miles is nothing on this drivetrain. 9 miles from Media, easy to see. Parts are everywhere and cheap.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate top completely — check all seals especially rear corners. Inspect rear seat for water staining. Check for T-3650 third-gear synchro if manual (slow-shift to 3rd). SN-197 convertible rear seat water intrusion is the main gotcha.
picks.bottomLine: Bulletproof American muscle droptop — the low-stress pick of the bunch.

picks.whyGood: 45k miles on a 2004 — genuinely exceptional for a 20-year-old cabriolet. B6 A4 Cabrio is elegant, well-built, and the 1.8T is a tuneable workhorse. Good Deal rating. Clean title, no accidents. This is the low-mileage sleeper of the list.
picks.whatToVerify: Hydraulic soft-top: listen for pump whine, check all four corners of the header seal. 1.8T timing belt service history is critical (belt, tensioner, water pump). Check for oil sludge if synthetic oil history is unclear. Inspect the folding top frame for cracks.
picks.bottomLine: Lowest-mileage cabriolet in the dataset — a gem if the top and timing belt are sorted.

picks.whyGood: Yes, 177k miles — but it's a MIATA. The NC Miata (2006-2015) routinely runs 200k+ with basic maintenance. Grand Touring trim adds leather, Bose audio, and a power hardtop (PRHT). Great Deal rating. Miatas don't depreciate much further, they're simply fun, and parts are everywhere.
picks.whatToVerify: Power retractable hardtop: cycle it 3 times, listen for binding or motor strain. Check for oil consumption (acceptable up to 1qt/3k). Inspect rear suspension bushings (common wear at 150k+). Timing chain cover seal. Cat-back for any rust.
picks.bottomLine: The purist choice: yes it has miles, but it's a Miata — these don't die, they just get more character.

picks.whyGood: R57 Cooper S brings the supercharged 1.6T — notchy gearbox, go-kart handling, big fun. 65k miles is modest. Under $8,500. Fair Deal rating. MINI Cooper S is genuinely one of the most enjoyable cheap convertibles available.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft top: inspect all seams and rear window bonding — R57 tops age. Timing chain tensioner failure is the main R56/R57 risk — ask for service records. Check for power steering fluid leaks. Water pump is electric, check for fault codes.
picks.bottomLine: Go-kart fun for under $8,500 — just confirm the timing chain is happy.

picks.whyGood: E36/7 Z3 2.3 hits the sweet spot: 2.5L straight-six (later than the 1.9L four), beautiful roadster proportions, simple enough to wrench on. 75k miles, Good Deal rating, at a real BMW specialist (Selden Motors has a good reputation). Clean title, no accidents.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft top: check header seals at windshield, rear window delamination. VANOS unit health on M52 engine. Cooling system — flush and inspect hoses. Check rear subframe for cracks (rare on 2.3 but possible). Inspect door seals.
picks.bottomLine: Classic roadster, honest price, reputable dealer — a solid safe pick.

picks.whyGood: Newest Mustang in the dataset. S550 EcoBoost Premium — 310 hp turbo four, modern safety tech, MyFord Touch infotainment, excellent build quality. 79k miles on a 2019 is nothing. EcoBoost with a tune delivers serious fun. At the top of the budget but it's the most modern car here by 4+ years.
picks.whatToVerify: EcoBoost: check for carbon buildup on intake valves (port injection issue — walnut blast if not done). Soft top: full cycle, inspect all seals. Check intercooler for leaks. Verify warranty transfer status.
picks.bottomLine: Most car per dollar when modernity matters — the EcoBoost S550 is genuinely excellent.

picks.whyGood: A Porsche Boxster in Media for $11,250 — that's the dream. Mid-engine balance, Porsche handling, 2.7L flat-six at 11 miles from home. The 986 Boxster (1997-2004) is a bargain performance car. 74k miles is relatively low. HOWEVER — verify IMS bearing service or upgrade.
picks.whatToVerify: IMS bearing is the make-or-break item: ask for documentation of IMS bearing replacement or upgrade (LN Engineering retrofit kit). Without it, budget $1-2k for the fix. Also check: RMS rear main seal, coolant expansion tank condition, soft-top header seal. Have a Porsche indie PPI this car.
picks.bottomLine: The most rewarding car on this list IF — and only if — the IMS bearing is documented or you budget for the fix.

mustangs.introHtml: All Ford Mustang convertibles within 35 miles, ranked for V8 grunt, transmission feel, and long-term reliability.

picks.whyGood: Newest Mustang convertible in the search. S550 platform, EcoBoost turbo four at 310 hp, excellent refinement. 79k miles is young for a 2019. Closest to a modern driver's car on this list.
picks.whatToVerify: EcoBoost carbon buildup on intake valves — ask if walnut blast done. Full top cycle. Intercooler integrity. Any tune voids powertrain warranty remnant.
picks.bottomLine: Best modern Mustang drop-top in budget — well worth the drive to Plymouth Meeting.

picks.whyGood: Good Deal rated 2018 EcoBoost Premium — essentially the same car as the 2019 at $1,300 less, but 63k more miles. S550 platform is excellent. At $13k it's hard to argue with.
picks.whatToVerify: 141k is the high-water mark here — check EcoBoost for oil consumption, carbon buildup. Top seal condition (rear corners especially). Timing chain stretch codes.
picks.bottomLine: Great value if mileage doesn't concern you — EcoBoost is durable well past 100k.

picks.whyGood: V6 3.7L Cyclone is the reliability king of this group. 305 hp is genuinely quick. Only 9 miles from Media. 96k on a Cyclone V6 is nothing. Easy to service, parts everywhere.
picks.whatToVerify: Top seals at rear corners (S197 known water intrusion point). Rear seat for staining. Manual: T-3650 third-gear synchro test. Auto: shift quality.
picks.bottomLine: Most reliable Mustang drop-top on the list and closest to home.

picks.whyGood: 44k original miles on a 1994 GT Convertible — extraordinary. V8 5.0L HO. Clean title, no accidents. SN-95 first year with the GT40 heads and improved suspension. This is a future classic with legitimate low mileage.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 frame rails at convertible top mount-to-rocker junction — poke for rust. Top condition (30-year-old fabric). Verify miles with Carfax. Rubber seals will all need replacement at this age regardless of miles.
picks.bottomLine: 44k miles on a '94 GT V8 is unicorn territory — this one deserves a thorough look.

picks.whyGood: Great Deal rated '95 GT with 55k miles under $10k. Another low-mileage SN-95 V8 convertible. GT Motors is a specialty shop that knows classic Mustangs. Under $10k for a well-preserved V8 drop-top.
picks.whatToVerify: Same SN-95 rust check: frame at top mount, rocker panels, wheel wells. Top frame integrity and fabric condition. 5.0L HO: check for oil leaks at intake manifold (common), head gaskets if overheated.
picks.bottomLine: Best-priced V8 Mustang in the group — Great Deal rating from AutoTrader confirms it.
